what_changed: A ranking of criteria by form score emerged, with information (0.991) and causal (0.918) scoring highest and predictive (0.140) scoring lowest.
Increasing removal fraction caused delta_C and overshoot to increase monotonically, with system collapse appearing at the 50% threshold.
A repeating cycle of object-to-mapping-to-object shifts emerged across the sequence, confirming cyclical rather than hierarchical progression.
All 8 measurement variants predicted the same outcome significantly (p<0.05), with PC1 capturing 95.5% of variance, confirming robustness across methods.
Detected feature presence varied greatly by data format (CV up to 1.297), showing the encoding choice affects what features are found more than the underlying system.
Every audit shifted interpretation (100% shift rate), but a fixed-methodology null model produced zero shifts, tying shifts directly to methodology changes.
Every single interpretive shift was accompanied by element links; no shift occurred in their absence, establishing them as a necessary condition.
Most audits (20/26) reached level 3 retention where higher-order forms preserve lower-order ones; the five failures lacked this retention.
SP was binary (1.0 or 2.0), not continuous, and remained uniform across different system types while varying only with estimator bin count.
The four transition points formed a strict linear dependency chain: difference -> limitation -> retention -> recursion, each requiring the prior ones.
Model B (element-link-centric) scored highest overall (78 total), but Model C (experience-centric) uniquely explained retention and self-emergence.
The causal criterion failed in 6 of 10 systems (60% failure rate), while all other criteria succeeded universally, showing causality is not context-free.
Difference detection emerged in 56.9% of systems; ablation showed nonlinearity and memory were essential for emergence (survival ratios 0.021 and 0.000).
All 4 transition points appeared in all 11 construction sequences (100% each), confirming path independence of the transition structure.
All 7 sector combinations were classified steady or bounded, with metrics (EU, CR, IRPC) remaining high even at extreme recursion depths.
Shifts occurred in 100% of audits containing both actual element links and consistency; removing either component eliminated predictive power.
Basic forces stayed constant across all 7 transitions while the element graph grew monotonically (node count +1 to +4, edge count +1 to +10 per step).
At every transition, enormous difference space was available but only a tiny discrete subset was retained; selection and retention, not creation, drove capability emergence.
Only 18 of 1200 systems (1.5%) met all criteria; the best composite score was 0.7267 with 8 symbols, showing minimal rule sets can generate all properties.
Density manipulation strongly predicted structural properties (NN distance R²=0.94) but did not significantly predict pre_C (R²=0.055, p=0.070).
